Details: Removal of fluoride through lime softening has been found to be marginally effective (0 - 80% removal) in both bench and full scale applications but is highly dependent on the amount of magnesium removed during the softening process.  If sufficient amounts of magnesium are not present in water, a magnesium salt would need to be added to provide the desired level of fluoride removal.  This method may be adaptable to low-fluoride-high-magnesium waters requiring softening.  The effectiveness of lime softening on fluoride removal is highly dependent on source water quality conditions.
